modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 12 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Identify common signs and symptoms of conditions associated with patient/resident distress and describe the Nurse Assistant's role and responsibility in preventing and/or responding.
Describe the immediate interventions in a medical emergency.
List the causes and signs of choking and discuss the use of abdominal thrusts for relief of obstructed airway.
Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. This module may be taught concurrently with Module 4: Safe Environment

cards.learning_goal: Describe the immediate interventions in a medical emergency.
cards.learner_facing_content: Source-traced focus: Describe the immediate interventions in a medical emergency.. Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: for role in medical emergency C. Cerebrovascular accident (CVA, Stroke, or brain attack) 1. A disturbance or obstruction of the flow of blood to a particular area of the brain with subsequent death of tissue 2. Signs and Symptoms a. Hemiplegia or weakness of one side of the body or numbness or tingling on one side of the body b. Aphasia-difficulty in speaking or understanding speech c. Headache d. Vision changes, blurred vision, pupils unequal e. Facial changes 1) Cheeks may "puff "on exhalation

cards.learning_goal: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. This module may be taught concurrently with Module 4: Safe Environment
cards.learner_facing_content: Source-traced focus: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. This module may be taught concurrently with Module 4: Safe Environment. Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: Describe common emergency codes used in long-term care facilities. A. Emergency code colors and meanings 1. Code Red � fire 2. Code Blue � adult medical emergency (Cardiac or Respiratory) 3. Code Yellow �bomb threat 4. Code Gray � combative person 5. Code Silver � person with weapon or hostage 6. Code Orange � hazardous waste spill or release B. Codes may vary according to facility C. Observe special consideration for hearing and sight impaired patients/residents Module 12: Emergency Procedures
